title: Rich Tender Middle Eastern Lamb Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic stew originating from North Africa, featuring tender lamb, whole wheat, and a blend of exotic spices, perfect for a low-sodium diet. This dish is slow-cooked to perfection in a traditional clay pot with a conical lid, allowing the flavors to meld together.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven or clay tagine, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger, and cook for an additional minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, smoked paprika, and dried apricots, and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
About 30 minutes before serving, prepare the whole wheat couscous according to package instructions.
To serve, place the couscous on a large platter, and spoon the lamb and sauce over the top. Garnish with chopped parsley.
